holdings.infoBox: FGM is an equity ETF with a total of 43 individual holdings. The top holdings are Aurubis AG stock at 6.68%, HOCHTIEF Aktiengesellschaft at 5.95%, Schaeffler AG at 4.63%, Nordex SE at 4.48%, and Deutsche Lufthansa AG at 4.27%.

overview.description: The First Trust Germany AlphaDEX Fund (FGM) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the NASDAQ AlphaDEX Germany index. The fund tracks a tiered equal-weighted index of 40 companies selected from the S&P Germany BMI based on the S&P's AlphaDex selection methodology. FGM was launched on Feb 14, 2012 and is issued by First Trust.

data.title: German industry employment falls to ten-year low, study shows
data.text: The number of people employed in ​German industry in 2025 fell to a ‌10-year low of just 6.6 million workers, a study by the German ​Economic Institute (IW) showed on ​Thursday.

data.text: Investor confidence improved unexpectedly sharply on hopes the conflict in the Middle East would end soon and energy prices would drop.

data.title: A mammoth economic stimulus passed last year in Germany was meant to jolt the country—and Europe—out of its economic slumber. The problem: Germans just aren't good at spending money.
data.text: While much of the rest of the developed world is swimming in too much debt, Germany is having trouble using just a tiny bit more.

data.title: German cabinet approves health insurance reform to curb costs
data.text: Germany's cabinet approved a ​draft bill to reform ‌the statutory health insurance system on Wednesday, aiming to ​save 16.3 billion euros ($19.08 ​billion) next year amid mounting ⁠costs.
